title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion
notes: No music. Once player reaches in-game, performance becomes very choppy, even at low settings. Crashes closed within 5 seconds of in-game.

title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion
notes:
UPDATE: ALL PROBLEMS FIXED!
From Proton GitHub: Install this missing dependency...
sudo apt install libmpg123-0:i386
Perfect performance now!!

title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition
notes: Need to set your real screen resolution in launcher or otherwise it won't run. OBSE fails to work so you can't run most of your usual mods. Minor mouse issue in menus or character dialog menus.

title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: It runs, but at 20fps, with minimum or maxed settings it sticks to 20fps. I'm going to say that with a 1080ti, it's not due to lacking hardware.
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: Gameplay is smooth, everything works, except it detects a non-existent joystick and I'm stuck running forward and left unless joysticks are disabled in the .ini file.
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: Played through the tutorial. 60 fps constantly
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: Tested playing around with existing 100-hour save and spin up a brand new game through to the end of the tutorial. Game seems to run almost flawlessly, only point of concern is some minor choppiness when running with high anti-aliasing on, lowering that fixes it up but is still very much playable at high AA.
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: The has terrible FPS out of the box, but after running "apt install libmpg123-0:i386" it worked like a charm. I got 35~80 FPS on the world map and 100+ FPS inside towns.

title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: The game itself runs flawlessly with consistent framerate. Minor lighting issues in exceedingly dark dungeons. Only had one crash due to alt-tab.

title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: Had no issues at all running the game from a clean install. Same performance as on Windows within a frame or two.
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: After a while it develops audio glitched on NPC speech that can only be resolved by rebooting the game, a few items have glitched icons in the inventory menu and mods don't appear to work, at least the ones that I've tried.
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: Everything seems to work. It may be that after some hours of gameplay the audio starts to stutter, but it might've been just my computer's fault. Either way, restarting the game solved it.
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: Needed to install 'lib32-mpg123' for it to work properly, after which it runs perfectly.
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes:
Changing settings, starting a new game and saving it, reloading the saved game after a fresh restart, and even reloading an old save from Windows, everything works as if it was a native game.
The game works fine with Ultra High setup, except HDR which is not compatible with the Intel driver. Anti-aliasing works, but with a big slow down
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: Installed 'lib32-mpg123' as per the other reports and the game works perfectly. I've managed to install mods with mixed results.
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: Save and reload after restart works. The game runs smoothly (about 30fps) in ultra details mode, either with HDR or Bloom + FSAAx4. I did a ride all around the main city with almost no freezes during map changes. Even Steam cloud works better than certain native games, as I could save the game on one computer and reload it without trouble on another one. I don't have time to play the game further, but it seems working as nicely as native to me
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: install lib32-mpg123 and everything runs great aside from the random crashes the occur once every 3-4 hours
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: Runs perfectly, had no crashes in the two hours I played. I did install lib32-mpg123.
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: It runs... but it is really slow.... I was using the latest version of wine it was playing at 60+ fps. and with the default Steam Proton it is playing at 15fps or lower,  combat would make it unplayable.
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: I restored an old savegame and rode all around the land without any trouble. All GFX preferences including HDR, bloom and FSAA until x16 works pefectly. As far as I tested it, the games feels like native
